Title: Queryable Mode - invalid and outdated info
Body:
**[Summary]** This ticket addresses inaccuracies in the DevExpress documentation regarding the Queryable mode in Blazor applications. The default data access mode for lookup List Views in XAF Blazor is Client, not Queryable. Additionally, the claim that only `LookupPropertyEditor` supports this mode is outdated as `DxTreeListEditor` also supports it. The proposed solution is to update the help topic to ensure the information is accurate.

**[Subject]** Queryable Mode - invalid and outdated info **[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1313897: Blazor - Lookup Property Editor does not display dynamic Default Member]({'FriendlyId':'T1313897','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

The following information is incorrect:

> The **Queryable** mode is the default mode in lookup List Views in ASP.NET Core Blazor applications.

The default data access more in lookup List View in XAF Blazor is Client. The Queryable mode is used in lookup List Views if the corresponding List View node of the Application Model specifies any data access mode other than Client.

> Only `LookupPropertyEditor` supports this mode.

This information is outdated. `DxTreeListEditor` also supports the Queryable mode, which is already reflected in our other help topics:

* [List View Data Access Modes](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113683/ui-construction/views/list-view-data-access-modes)
* [DxTreeListEditor Class](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/DevExpress.ExpressApp.Blazor.Editors.DxTreeListEditor)

## Solution

Modify the help topic to reflect the correct information. **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]** [https://github.com/DevExpress/dxvcs/issues/21570](https://github.com/DevExpress/dxvcs/issues/21570)

Title: TreeList - Drag information is not recalculated when the Handled property is set to true
Body:
**[Summary]** The issue described involves the DragOver event not recalculating event arguments in the DragDropBehavior of TreeList when the Handled property is set to true. The solution is to call `e.Default` within the DragOver event to ensure that automatic calculation occurs properly in scenarios where Handled is true.

**[Subject]** TreeList - Drag information is not recalculated when the Handled property is set to true 

**[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1320002: TreeList - Drag information is not recalculated when the Handled property is set to true]({'FriendlyId':'T1320002','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

It is not described how to properly handle the DragOver event for DragDropBehavior. The issue is that with certain implementations, the user expects that automatic calculation of event arguments for DragOver occur the next time it triggers when **Handled** event argument is set to true.

## Solution

Add a note or remark that it is necessary to call `e.Default` in the DragOver event to trigger calculation again in usage scenarios where user set the Handled event argument to true. In short, call `e.Default` first before overriding event arguments as automatic calculation does not happen until after DragOver.

## Related Help Topics

[Drag-and-Drop Behavior](https://docs.devexpress.com/WindowsForms/118656/common-features/behaviors/drag-and-drop-behavior)

Title: Mail Merge - missing crucial XAF Blazor details
Body:
**[Summary]**  
The customer inquired about the support for subfields in the Mail Merge feature of XAF Blazor, leading to a lengthy discussion between the XAF and ASP teams. It was confirmed that the underlying [DxRichEdit](https://docs.devexpress.com/Blazor/DevExpress.Blazor.RichEdit.DxRichEdit) component does not support subfields, particularly in the preview mode. However, XAF’s "Show In Document" Action utilizes the API from [XtraRichEdit.RichEditDocumentServer](https://docs.devexpress.com/OfficeFileAPI/DevExpress.XtraRichEdit.RichEditDocumentServer), allowing the Mail Merge to function correctly in that context. A solution was proposed to note the limitations clearly.

**[Subject]** Mail Merge - missing crucial XAF Blazor details **[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1318982: Re: Mail Merge Module in XAF Blazor doesn't display subfields values]({'FriendlyId':'T1318982','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

The customer came asking whether the subfields are now supported for Mail Merge. This resulted in spending a lot of time of going back and forth between XAF and ASP teams.

The reality of the situation is that the underlying [DxRichEdit](https://docs.devexpress.com/Blazor/DevExpress.Blazor.RichEdit.DxRichEdit) component does not support subfields (confirmed by the devs, see [this thread](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/message/19:d42957ec16674620a98be7e0c61b5db8@thread.skype/1768989580744?tenantId=e4d60396-9352-4ae8-b84c-e69244584fa4&groupId=ae1fc000-d607-48ea-a337-9afea6d92bd0&parentMessageId=1768989580744&teamName=Support&channelName=ASP%20(WebForms%2C%20MVC%2C%20Bootstrap%2C%20Blazor)&createdTime=1768989580744)). Thus, subfields are not supported in the preview mode (the "View Merged Data" button). This limitation is described in the following help topic: [Mail Merge in Blazor Rich Text Editor](https://docs.devexpress.com/Blazor/403862/components/rich-edit/mail-merge) > [Step 1\. Specify a Data Source](https://docs.devexpress.com/Blazor/403862/components/rich-edit/mail-merge#step-1-specify-a-data-source):

> The Rich Text Editor cannot get data from a nested field. If your data source contains complex fields, modify the data source before binding.

However, XAF's "Show In Document" Action internally uses the API of [XtraRichEdit.RichEditDocumentServer](https://docs.devexpress.com/OfficeFileAPI/DevExpress.XtraRichEdit.RichEditDocumentServer) to create a merged document for display, which makes the results of the Mail Merge feature work correctly in XAF Blazor, despite the Blazor component's limitations.

## Solution

Make a note describing it as it is - in XAF Blazor subfields work in the "Show In Document" Action, but not in preview mode. **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]** Created a card for docs [https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=151091814&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C20059](https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=151091814&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C20059)

Title: Incorrect info about Standalone Model Editor in Blazor
Body:
**[Summary]** The issue reported pertains to incorrect instructions for using the Standalone Model Editor with Blazor applications. Currently, the documentation suggests selecting configuration files, which is not applicable for Blazor apps. Instead, it requires selecting the *MySolution.Blazor.Server.dll* file. The proposed solution involves creating distinct instructions for WinForms and Blazor applications, clarifying that for deployed Blazor apps, selecting module assemblies in the initial dialog does not work as intended.


**[Subject]** Incorrect info about Standalone Model Editor in Blazor  
**[Description]** ## Issue

It says:

>To edit the Application Model of the deployed application, you should specify the configuration file name only. An option to choose an assembly file and model differences path allows you to edit the model of a specific module outside of Visual Studio.

However, this is not applicable to Blazor applications. In Blazor apps, you need to select the *MySolution.Blazor.Server.dll* file (there is no \*.config file to select, and if you select a module assembly file such as *MySolution.Module.dll*, changes will be saved to *Model.DesignedDiffs.xafml* instead of *Model.xafml*).

## Solution

Write separate instructions for WinForms and Blazor. Note that for deployed apps, it does not make sense to select module assemblies in the initial dialog of Standalone Model Editor, because modules' *Model.DesignedDiffs.xafml* files are already compiled to embedded resources in this case. The only viable option is to select the config file for WinForms and the *MySolution.Blazor.Server.dll* file for Blazor.

## Related Help Topics

Instructions about Standalone Model Editor are also given in [Allow an Administrator to Create Custom Persistent Fields in an XPO-Based Application](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113649/business-model-design-orm/types-info-subsystem/allow-an-administrator-to-create-custom-persistent-fields-in-an-xpo-based-application) and [Use Metadata to Customize Business Classes Dynamically](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113583/business-model-design-orm/types-info-subsystem/use-metadata-to-customize-business-classes-dynamically). **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]** [https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=149975388&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C19437](https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=149975388&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C19437)

Title: How to: Choose the Optimal Query Splitting Behavior in Entity Framework Core | XAF: Cross-Platform .NET App UI & Web API | DevExpress Documentation
Body:
**[Summary]** The ticket raises an issue regarding the documentation on optimal query splitting behavior in Entity Framework Core related to the `UseConnectionString()` method. The customer notes that while the approach to remove database-specific code on startup is beneficial, it also leads to concerns about its inadequacy for real-world applications. Specifically, the documentation does not provide clear instructions for reliably utilizing split query mode, which results in warning messages during startup. The customer requests improvements to the documentation to address these deficiencies.

**[Subject]** How to: Choose the Optimal Query Splitting Behavior in Entity Framework Core | XAF: Cross-Platform .NET App UI & Web API | DevExpress Documentation 

**[Description]** Feedback on [https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/404862/business-model-design-orm/business-model-design-with-entity-framework-core/performance/how-to-choose-optimal-query-splitting-behavior](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/404862/business-model-design-orm/business-model-design-with-entity-framework-core/performance/how-to-choose-optimal-query-splitting-behavior)

> Was this page helpful?

Yes, but...

> Add or update sample code.

> Add information/instructions.

> What can we do to improve this page?

This highlights a pretty big problem with the new 25.2 `UseConnectionString()` method - it removes database-specific code from startup (that's great!), but this article shows that knowing your database provider is necessary so that you can call the right `Use[DB-Specific-Method]()`. 

Every new XAF app using EFCore on a relational database using the Security module logs a warning on every startup saying you should probably be using split query mode.

But this doc doesn't show how to do that reliably - it's been adjusted to be _valid_ for 25.2 and `UseConnectionString()`, but I wouldn't call it "correct".

I think the real issue is that `UseConnectionString()` is insufficient for real-world apps. Please fix that!

Title: You have a new 'GitHub Examples — User Feedback' response.
Body:
**[Summary]** The customer provided feedback on a GitHub example related to DevExpress UI components, specifically addressing issues with role name sanitization in C#. The problem identified was that role names with special characters were causing compilation errors. The proposed solution included implementing a sanitization mechanism to create valid C# identifiers while preserving original role names for database operations. The changes made include the addition of a dictionary for mapping sanitized names, modifications to the `RoleGenerator.cs` and `UpdaterRuntimeTemplate.tt` for name handling, and regeneration of the template with the new properties. Overall, the solution ensures compatibility and proper functionality without breaking existing code.

**[Subject]** You have a new 'GitHub Examples — User Feedback' response. **[Description]** ### Example 
 [XAF_How-to-get-role-code-from-the-UI](https://github.com/DevExpress-Examples/XAF_How-to-get-role-code-from-the-UI) 

### How long have you been using DevExpress UI components/development libraries?
 
 Over 6 Years
### was-helpful 
 yes
### What problems/issues did the sample help address or what questions did it help answer?  Do you have additional suggestions/comments for future content/examples?
 
 Additional functionality I did develop which I think could improve this solution/example:
# Role Name Sanitization

## Problem
When generating role updater code, role names containing special characters (spaces, parentheses, etc.) like "Functioneel Beheer (WLNMobile)" were used directly as C# method names, causing compilation errors.

## Solution
Implemented a sanitization mechanism that:

1. **Sanitizes role names** for use as valid C# identifiers by replacing non-alphanumeric characters with underscores
2. **Preserves original role names** for database lookups and role.Name assignments
3. **Maintains backward compatibility** with existing code structure

## Changes Made

### 1. RoleGenerator.cs
- Added `sanitizedRoleNames` dictionary to map sanitized names to original names
- Added `SanitizeRoleName()` method that:
  - Ensures first character is a letter or underscore
  - Replaces all non-alphanumeric characters (except underscores) with underscores
  - Handles null/empty names gracefully
- Updated `GetUpdaterCode()` to:
  - Generate sanitized names for each role
  - Store the mapping between sanitized and original names
  - Pass the mapping to the template

### 2. UpdaterRuntimeTemplate.tt
- Added `SanitizedRoleNames` property to receive the name mapping
- Updated template logic to:
  - Use sanitized names for method names (e.g., `CreateFunctioneel_Beheer__WLNMobile_Role()`)
  - Use original names for database lookups (e.g., `new BinaryOperator("Name", "Functioneel Beheer (WLNMobile)")`)

### 3. UpdaterRuntimeTemplate.cs
- Regenerated from the .tt template with the new property and logic

## Example

**Input Role Name:** `"Functioneel Beheer (WLNMobile)"`

**Generated Method:**
```csharp
public void CreateFunctioneel_Beheer__WLNMobile_Role() {
    PermissionPolicyRole role = ObjectSpace.FindObject<PermissionPolicyRole>(
        new BinaryOperator("Name", "Functioneel Beheer (WLNMobile)"));
    if(role == null) {
        role = ObjectSpace.CreateObject<PermissionPolicyRole>();
        role.Name = "Functioneel Beheer (WLNMobile)";
        // ... rest of role configuration
    }
}
```

## Benefits
- ? Valid C# method names are generated
- ? Original role names are preserved in the database
- ? No breaking changes to existing functionality
- ? Handles any special characters in role names **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]** [https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=150400675&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C19594](https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=150400675&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C19594) [<PERSON>: ??? ?????? ????????? ?? ?????? **[Summary]**  
The customer reported a potential memory leak issue related to the `ObjectSpace_Disposed` method in the `NonPersistentObjectSpaceExtender` class documentation. The specific issue is that the method does not remove all the event handlers added in the constructor. The customer suggests that the missing line to unsubscribe from this event should be included to prevent memory leaks.

**[Subject]** How to: Perform CRUD Operations with Non-Persistent Objects | XAF: Cross-Platform .NET App UI & Web API | DevExpress Documentation  
**[Description]** Feedback on [https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/115672/business-model-design-orm/non-persistent-objects/how-to-perform-crud-operations-with-non-persistent-objects](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/115672/business-model-design-orm/non-persistent-objects/how-to-perform-crud-operations-with-non-persistent-objects)

> Was this page helpful?

Yes, but...

> What can we do to improve this page?

In the listing for `NonPersistentObjectSpaceExtender`, the method `ObjectSpace_Disposed` method doesn't remove all the event handlers that were added in the constructor, which could result in a memory leak. Specifically it is missing:

```
objectSpace.Disposed -= ObjectSpace_Disposed;
```

Title: Need to rewrite code in the TreeList - Flat data source help topic
Missing one step of the process - Implement a Property Editor Based on a Custom Component (Blazor)
Body:
**[Summary]**  
Multiple customers have experienced confusion while implementing a custom control based on existing instructions for a Razor component (`InputText`). The existing help topic fails to demonstrate the necessary steps for customers creating their own Razor pages. To address this issue, two potential solutions are proposed: enhancing the current help topic with a simple demonstration of a custom Razor wrapper page, or illustrating an advanced usage scenario using a task from the XAF Blazor course that implements a custom Property Editor with a DevExtreme component. Further discussions on the organization of the help topics are recommended.

**[Subject]** Missing one step of the process - Implement a Property Editor Based on a Custom Component (Blazor) **[Description]** ## Issue

Multiple customers (not too many though) experienced an issue understanding the process of implementing a custom control while following the instructions from this help topic. The reason for that lies in the fact that the help topic's description based on an already existing Razor component (`InputText`) and relies on our inner mechanisms to tie Component Model to the Razor component's parameters.

However, that approach is not applicable when a customer creates a custom Razor page. In that case, the customer needs to specify parameters manually and tie them to the Razon control in code, which our help topic doesn't demonstrate.

## Solution 1 (simple):
Enhance the help topic to implement the `InputText` control through a custom Razor wrapper page for demonstration purposes.

## Solution 2 (advanced):
We can use this chance to illustrate a more advanced usage scenario and use the existing task from the advanced XAF Blazor course: [https://progress.hes.dev/Edit/Task/3ae74a60-c0d3-4ff9-97fb-2513728f0bf0?contentType=Task](https://progress.hes.dev/Edit/Task/3ae74a60-c0d3-4ff9-97fb-2513728f0bf0?contentType=Task).

This task's solution illustrate an implementation of our DevExtreme `dxColorBox` JS component as a custom Property Editor in XAF. It addresses more usage scenario and can potentially be more useful to the customers (but at the cost of reduced simplicity).

## Tickets:

After a quick search (in order not to spend much time on this), I could find a couple of tickets where the customers expressed their confusion and drawn our support resources to get a more thorough explanation:

* [Xaf - Blazor - Custom property editor]({'FriendlyId':'T1255597','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})
* (an extra-long thread) [How to migrate from adapters to version 24.]({'FriendlyId':'T1255142','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})
* [XAF Blazor: Custom LookupPropertyEditor or Ability to Add an Existing LookupPropertyEditor to Custom Layout Page]({'FriendlyId':'T1288145','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})
* [HyperlinkPropertyEditor in a ListView for NonPersistent Properties]({'FriendlyId':'T1266895','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'}) **[Comments]**  **[Answer]** I checked the tickets and proposals, and I do not like extending the existing topic, because it will be a mess. Instead, I prefer to have 2 more separate topics or 1 new topic with the 2 sections about a Razor file and the other about a JS custom control. I previously asked to build the demo exactly to demonstrate such scenarios - see my links in [{'FriendlyId':'T1255142','HashCode':'20284685-6fcd-425c-94e7-1994ec875ab1','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'}]({'FriendlyId':'T1255142','HashCode':'20284685-6fcd-425c-94e7-1994ec875ab1','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'}).

Ask <PERSON> for opinions as well, please. **[Note]** [https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=140685092&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C13676](https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=140685092&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C13676)

Title: Detrimental information - Calculate a Property Value Based on Values from a Detail Collection
Body:
**[Summary]** 
The ticket addresses concerns regarding a help section in the DevExpress documentation that provides an unreliable solution for calculating property values based on detail collections. The user recommends either deleting the help section or adding clarifying notes from a team member regarding the limitations of persistent calculated fields. A few potential solutions are presented which include using non-persistent calculated fields or defining computed properties at the database level.

**[Subject]** Detrimental information - Calculate a Property Value Based on Values from a Detail Collection **[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1314091: Optimistic Lock at Business Logic (Setter Property) base on Calculate-a-property-value-based-on-values-from-a-detail-collection]({'FriendlyId':'T1314091','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

We have the following help section: [Store Calculated Property Values in the Database](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113179/business-model-design-orm/business-model-design-with-xpo/calculate-a-property-value-based-on-values-from-a-detail-collection#store-calculated-property-values-in-the-database).

This help section contains an unreliable solution that is prone to causing issues. That help section was added for a very rare use case.

More info in this thread (in Russian): [link](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/message/19:35bcf1da713c4dc1955d2dd37af504da@thread.skype/1763627517660?tenantId=e4d60396-9352-4ae8-b84c-e69244584fa4&groupId=d06f3b24-2515-4dd5-8bda-bd37c414c600&parentMessageId=1763625521788&teamName=App%20Frameworks%20%28UI%2C%20API%2C%20ORM%29&channelName=!Core-Security-Data&createdTime=1763627517660).

## Solution

There are the following possible solutions:

1. Delete the aforementioned help section entirely.
2. Add a **red note** based on the following comment from <PERSON>:

> Also, a few notes on persistent calculated fields: this is also not the first option for your task, because it has obvious limitations (you have to update the fields all the time manually to see actual values). I recommend you use non-persistent calculated fields in the first place, and the simplest code from [PersistentAliasAttribute](https://docs.devexpress.com/XPO/DevExpress.Xpo.PersistentAliasAttribute). Alternatively, you can define computed properties at the database level using [FetchOnlyAttribute](https://docs.devexpress.com/XPO/DevExpress.Xpo.FetchOnlyAttribute). With both options, you will always have the actual values evaluated on the database server side.
> 
> More complicated solutions for non-persistent calculated fields from [Calculate a Property Value Based on Values from a Detail Collection](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113179/business-model-design-orm/business-model-design-with-xpo/calculate-a-property-value-based-on-values-from-a-detail-collection) is my 2nd choice, but again you will have to perform updates manually. This help topic demonstrates only a few popular cases with manual updates, and you may need to write additional code for other scenarios. **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]** ?????? ??????? ? 24.2+ ???????
[https://git.internal.devexpress.devx/Documentation/eXpressAppFramework/-/merge_requests/7701](https://git.internal.devexpress.devx/Documentation/eXpressAppFramework/-/merge_requests/7701)

Title: The translation dialog appears in browser, as the language is changed several seconds after app loading
Body:
**[Summary]**  
The issue described involves a translation dialog appearing in the browser when a user opens an XAF app due to a delay in the language setting from "en" to "cs". This occurs as the browser initially receives `<html lang="en"… />` content, which is later dynamically changed. The solution involves customizing the `<html>` tag in the `Pages\_Host.cshtml` file of the XAF Blazor application to specify the correct culture from the start, thus preventing the translation popup from appearing.

**[Subject]** The translation dialog appears in browser, as the language is changed several seconds after app loading **[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1312700: Annoying and confusing translation dialog]({'FriendlyId':'T1312700','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

When user opens XAF app, browser receives `<html lang="en"… />` content which is dynamically changed to lang="cs" in few seconds. In between browser shows the translation popup.

## Solution

Add information to the [Single language support](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/402956/localization/localize-an-xaf-application#single-language-support) section:

To resolve the issue, customize the `<html>` tag in the `Pages\_Host.cshtml` file In XAF Blazor application.  Specify the correct culture: `<html lang="cs"… />` . After that, the correct culture should be set since the initialization, and the browser translation popup shouldn't appear. **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]**

Title: Obsolete information about CreateDefaultObjectSpaceProvider
Body:
**[Summary]**  
The ticket addresses the outdated information regarding the `CreateDefaultObjectSpaceProvider` method in the context of XAF applications. The current practice is to configure Object Space Providers through the Application Builder in `Startup.cs`, rather than overriding the method in project templates. The solution involves removing obsolete information, directing users to a relevant help topic, and updating the note about the Application Builder.

**[Subject]** Obsolete information about CreateDefaultObjectSpaceProvider **[Description]** ## Issue

The topic says:

> By default, [XPObjectSpaceProvider](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/DevExpress.ExpressApp.Xpo.XPObjectSpaceProvider) is created because the WinForms and ASP.NET Web Forms application project templates override the **CreateDefaultObjectSpaceProvider** method of the corresponding **XafApplication** descendant in the following manner:

However, XAF applications created by project templates no longer override CreateDefaultObjectSpaceProvider. They initialize builder.ObjectSpaceProviders in Startup.cs (both Blazor and WinForms).

## Solution

1. Remove information about CreateDefaultObjectSpaceProvider. Describe that XAF applications configure Object Space Providers through the Application Builder (note that providers are configured slightly differently in EF Core and XPO, so do not be too specific).
2. Instead of suggesting CreateCustomObjectSpaceProvider, reference the following help topic: [Custom Object Space Provider](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/405388/data-manipulation-and-business-logic/custom-object-space-provider).
3. Rework the note about the Application Builder at the end of the topic. **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]** [https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=133772919&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C8430](https://github.com/orgs/DevExpress/projects/157/views/1?pane=issue&itemId=133772919&issue=DevExpress%7Cdxvcs%7C8430)

Title: Use the Security System - missing essential DbSets
Body:
**[Summary]** The issue reported pertains to the integration of the Security System in an XAF application, where end-user model differences were not properly documented, leading to exceptions during updates. The solution involves modifying the tutorial to include essential database storage for model differences.

**[Subject]** Use the Security System - missing essential DbSets 
**[Description]** ## Issue

When the Security System is used in XAF, end-user model differences must be [stored in the database](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113698/ui-construction/application-model-ui-settings-storage/application-model-storages/store-the-application-model-differences-in-the-database). The tutorial misses this information, which results in an XAF application throwing an exception when followed this tutorial and update the project from previous lessons.

## Solution

Add the following to Step 3 (the offered text below is a draft, please modify it however necessary).

To the description:

> When the Security System is used, end-user model differences must be [stored in the database](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113698/ui-construction/application-model-ui-settings-storage/application-model-storages/store-the-application-model-differences-in-the-database).

To the code snippet:

```cs
public DbSet<ModelDifference> ModelDifferences { get; set; }
public DbSet<ModelDifferenceAspect> ModelDifferenceAspects { get; set; }
```
**[Comments]**  
**[Answer]**  
**[Note]**

Title: Missing info about contradiction between Template Localization and Satellite Assemblies (Localization Service)
Body:
**[Summary]** The customer is experiencing confusion due to missing information in the help topics regarding the interaction between Template Localization and Satellite Assemblies in the localization process. Template Localization takes priority over Satellite Assemblies, which may lead to issues when attempting to partially localize controls through both methods. The suggested solution is to update the documentation to clarify this relationship.

**[Subject]** Missing info about contradiction between Template Localization and Satellite Assemblies (Localization Service) **[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1297360: Satellite resources "nl" is not consequently used by ASPxGridViewlocalizer]({'FriendlyId':'T1297360','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

There's a lack of information in our help topics regarding how two different ways of control localization interact with each other: 

1. Template Localization.
2. Satellite Assemblies (Localization Service).

Template Localization exports all localizable strings to the "Localization" node of the Application Model. This leads to XAF's localization mechanisms completely ignoring any data from Satellite Assemblies related to the control in question, because Application Model takes priority.

**IMPORANT:** This concerns even the values that have been exported but not localized in the Application Model. In other words, you cannot localize control partially through Template Localization and partially through Satellite Assemblies - the Template Localization mechanism will completely override Satellite Assemblies.

## Solution

Add the described information to the related help topics.

## Related Help Topics

* [How to: Localize a WinForms Template](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/114495/localization/how-to-localize-a-winforms-template)
* [Localize an XAF Application \(\.NET\)](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/402956/localization/localize-an-xaf-application)

Title: Describe new XAF0033 warning
Action hidden by a Conditional Appearance Rule appears as disabled in List Views
Body:
**[Summary]**  
The issue revolves around an action that is expected to be hidden by a Conditional Appearance Rule but is instead displayed as disabled in List Views. This behavior is by design to prevent overly complicated calculations that could impair application performance. The proposed solution is to add a note to the documentation regarding this behavior in the relevant sections.

**[Subject]** Action hidden by a Conditional Appearance Rule appears as disabled in List Views **[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1292192: Unable to Hide SimpleAction Using Conditional Appearance]({'FriendlyId':'T1292192','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

When an Action is **hidden** via Conditional Appearance Rule (e.g., the `Appearance` attribute), it is still visible in List Views and is displayed as **disabled** instead of **hidden**. This is by design, it is done to avoid overcomplicated calculations that would negatively impact the application's performance.

## Solution

We should add a note about this to the description of the 5th code snippet in the [Examples](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113371/conditional-appearance/declare-conditional-appearance-rules-in-code#examples) section. 

That note should also be duplicated here: [Example 3](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/DevExpress.ExpressApp.ConditionalAppearance.AppearanceAttribute.AppearanceItemType#example-3).

Title: FROM PM: Add a note about Visual Studio code style settings, which lead to runtime errors
Body:
**[Summary]** 
This ticket addresses an issue reported by two customers regarding runtime errors in their applications due to Visual Studio code style settings that affect the WinForms designer. The main problem is that the `this` access qualifier may be removed when forms are modified and saved in the designer, leading to runtime issues. The proposed solution is to add a note about the Visual Studio settings, explaining how to avoid these errors by referring to Microsoft's documentation on code style settings.

**[Subject]** FROM PM: Add a note about Visual Studio code style settings, which lead to runtime errors 

**[Description]** ## Issue

1. It was reported by two customers, the last time in [Form Designer Breaks form on any change]({'FriendlyId':'T1286701','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'}). 
2. Unfortunately, it is very difficult to diagnose and I myself had to consult with the WinForms team: [<PERSON>: ? InitializeComponent (XXX.designer.cs) ???????? WinForms ??????? ...](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/message/19:b4627de4721a4490a013e2cdfe7afa51@thread.skype/1744277860495?tenantId=e4d60396-9352-4ae8-b84c-e69244584fa4&groupId=66e1ce4e-a386-4ec2-95d6-8d0336a7da39&parentMessageId=1744277860495&teamName=WinForms&channelName=Developers!&createdTime=1744277860495) posted in WinForms / Developers! on Thursday, April 10, 2025 12:37 PM
3. This code style is enabled by default in Visual Studio (needs manual disable), so I expect more customers hit this, but just gave up without reporting.

## Solution

Add a red note 

>Microsoft WinForms designer code generation is fully controlled by your Visual Studio and project settings. If you modify and save your forms in the designer, the `this` access qualifier may be removed and runtime errors will occur as a result. For example, your DetailRibbonForm1.designer.cs file will define all components as "ribbonControl.XXX" instead of "this.ribbonControl.XXX". 
To return "this." and avoid runtime errors after designer modifications, please follow Microsoft docs at [Support for code style settings](https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/blob/main/docs/designer/modernization-of-code-behind-in-OOP-designer/modernization-of-code-behind-in-oop-designer.md#support-for-code-style-settings).

in the following topics:

[https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113706/ui-construction/templates/in-winforms/how-to-create-a-custom-winforms-standard-template](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113706/ui-construction/templates/in-winforms/how-to-create-a-custom-winforms-standard-template)

[https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/112618/ui-construction/templates/in-winforms/how-to-create-a-custom-winforms-ribbon-template](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/112618/ui-construction/templates/in-winforms/how-to-create-a-custom-winforms-ribbon-template)

Title: OAuth and Custom Authentication - a crucial aspect is not emphasized
Body:
**[Summary]** The issue highlights a missing detail in the documentation regarding the `NameClaimType` value in OAuth and Custom Authentication for applications using `DevExpress.ExpressApp.Security.UserManager`. It is critical for the `ClaimsIdentity.Name` value to be present when parsing a principal from a third-party OAuth Identity Provider. The proposed solution is to update the documentation to underscore this important requirement.

**[Subject]** OAuth and Custom Authentication - a crucial aspect is not emphasized

**[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1281075: I am trying to implement OKTA in my application.]({'FriendlyId':'T1281075','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

Help topics of this help section miss the crucial piece of information - the necessity to set the `NameClaimType` value. The reason for this is that our `DevExpress.ExpressApp.Security.UserManager` class expects the [ClaimsIdentity.Name](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.claims.claimsidentity.name?view=net-9.0#remarks) value to exist (e.g., to be not null) when parsing a principal from a third-party OAuth Identity Provider:

![Clipboard-File-1.png]({'Id':'c81040ef-0319-4a87-b6c5-f81b418e8781','CheckAnswerDesk':'5741edc0-3bab-43ab-8f0c-525a43474742'})

The `NameClaimType` property is briefly mentioned in one of the code snippets in the [Active Directory and OAuth2 Authentication Providers in WinForms Applications](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/404752/data-security-and-safety/security-system/authentication/oauth-and-custom-authentication/active-directory-and-oauth2-authentication-providers-in-winforms-applications) help topic, but no comment is given.

## Solution

Reflect this information in our documentation and emphasize its importance.

## Related Help Topics

(Microsoft) [Name claim and role claim mapping](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/security/authentication/claims?view=aspnetcore-9.0#name-claim-and-role-claim-mapping). **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]**

Title: [S ] Key Properties - EF Core string key property
Body:
**[Summary]** This ticket discusses the common issue of creating a custom string key property in Entity Framework Core and suggests enhancing the documentation to provide clarity on this process. It highlights specific error handling in XAF and references additional resources for further assistance.

**[Subject]** Key Properties - EF Core string key property **[Description]** [DevExpress Support Team: CLONED FROM [T1283038: XAF entity primary key issue consultation.]({'FriendlyId':'T1283038','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'})]

## Issue

The creation of a custom key property of the string type is a popular question that generates excessive support traffic. While the question itself is not entirely related to XAF, it would be beneficial to elaborate on this usage scenario in our documentation.

## Solution

In the [Entity Framework \(EF\)](https://docs.devexpress.com/eXpressAppFramework/113665/business-model-design-orm/data-types-supported-by-built-in-editors/key-properties#entity-framework-ef) section, add a sub-section that describes the task of creating a custom string key property.

The task itself is described in the following Stack Overflow thread: [How to use String property as primary key in Entity Framework](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/32983524/how-to-use-string-property-as-primary-key-in-entity-framework).

However, the following XAF specifics must be taken into consideration and described in the help topic as well: [EF Core -  A custom reference type key (e.g. a String key) may throw InvalidOperationException (Unable to track an entity of type X because its primary key property Y is null) at IObjectSpace.CreateObject]({'FriendlyId':'T1179042','HashCode':'','CheckAnswerDesk':'7bb212e9-1af8-4a62-a4b6-fd592cbadb44'}). **[Comments]**  **[Answer]**  **[Note]**
